entry.title: Washington Post: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Turkish strike kills at least 9 Iraqi tourists in northern resort
  • Amid summer heat wave, Germany worries about having enough gas for winter
  • Sri Lanka picks establishment figure as president, risking more unrest
  • Boris Johnson says ‘hasta la vista, baby!’ as party votes for his replacement
  • Italy’s Draghi open to staying on as prime minister — with conditions
  • Brutal heat dome moves east, with Central Europe set to swelter
  • Quidditch is now quadball, distancing game from J.K. Rowling, league says
  • Russian tennis star criticizes Ukraine war in rare dissent, comes out as gay
  • Ukraine’s farmers become the latest target of Russian missiles
  • E.U. proposes plan to ration gas ahead of ‘likely’ cutoff by Russia
  • Methanol found in bodies of 21 teens who died in South African nightclub
  • Ukraine live briefing: E.U. braces for ‘likely’ Russian gas cutoff; Putin suggests progress in grain talks
  • After 246 years, Marines set for their first Black four-star general
  • China has a hand in Sri Lanka’s economic calamity
  • Japan plans state funeral Sept. 27 for slain former leader Shinzo Abe
  • Russia seeks to annex Ukraine’s east, south later this year, U.S. says
  • Ukraine live briefing: White House warns of Moscow annexation plans; Russia and Ukraine undertake more diplomatic outreach
  • Michael Krepon, campaigner to limit nuclear arms, dies at 75
  • Keep cool and carry on? Britons struggle through hottest day on record.
  • Russia’s Gazprom sends force majeure letter to European clients
  • AP PHOTOS: Despair and poverty fuel drug use in Afghanistan
  • Official: Russia expanding its war focus to southern Ukraine
  • UK weather turmoil spurs calls to adapt to climate change
  • Libya says oil exports resumed after monthslong hiatus
  • Murdaugh pleads not guilty to murdering wife and son
  • She flew to Australia with a Subway sandwich. She was fined $1,844.
  • U.S. offers $5K to find wrongly freed dognapper of Lady Gaga’s Frenchies
  • She just got accepted to medical school. She’s 13.
  • A Missouri man is suing Bass Pro over ‘lifetime guarantee’ for socks
  • Law & Order crew member fatally shot at filming location in Brooklyn
  • Brett Kavanaugh’s neighbors: For abortion rights but tired of protests
  • Secret Service cannot recover texts; no new details for Jan. 6 committee
  • Post Politics Now: Biden to deliver climate ultimatum; House panel to weigh assault weapons ban
  • Steve Bannon trial live updates: Jan. 6 panel warned Bannon of possible charges, staffer testifies
  • Trump called ‘within the last week’ to overturn Wis. election results, speaker says
  • Trump’s choices set nation on path to Jan. 6 violence, committee shows
  • Post Politics Now: House passes measure that would codify same-sex marriage into law with bipartisan support
  • GOP fake electors ‘targets’ in Georgia election fraud inquiry
  • U.S. says Bannon ‘chose to show contempt’
  • Prosecutor: Steve Bannon thumbed his nose at the law
  • Opening statements soon in Bannon trial after judge rejects delay
  • Chipotle closes Maine store that sought to unionize
  • Amazon workers launch campaign to unionize in Albany
  • 6 key things to know about inflation-indexed bonds paying 9.62 percent
  • In ‘Endling: Extinction is Forever,’ witness climate doom through a fox’s eyes
  • Discord is coming to Xbox, but it won’t be one-click easy
  • FaZe Clan, the boisterous gaming lifestyle brand, goes public
  • Here’s how to keep your phone cool and working in a heat wave
  • A mid-century modern neighborhood ‘ensconced in nature’
  • New scoring system for condo and homeowner associations
  • The Earth Wants Biden to Keep Gas Prices High
  • Give Documented Dreamers a Chance to Stay
  • No, Joe Manchin, Taxes Don’t Cause Inflation
  • Retirement Expenses Are Too Hard to Predict
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • How Thailand Is Blazing Trails on Gay Rights, Legalizing Marijuana
  • Why Marburg Virus Is an Increasing Threat in Africa
  • How bad is BA.5? Dr. Leana Wen answers your questions.
  • Okay, but Cassandra cannot possibly be right *again*
  • The top Marine faces unprecedented opposition. He says that’s ‘positive.’
  • Biden has abdicated U.S. leadership on Syria to Russia and Iran
  • Democrats should take yes for an answer on Electoral Count Act reform
  • Shocker: Almost no one trusts TV news
  • How jailed Hong Kong protesters are subjected to ‘thought work’
  • What must still be faced in the Emmett Till tragedy
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 20 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-20 15:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Thai inmate serving sentence in Hong Kong repatriated, in first such transfer since agreement came into effect in 2000
  • Domestic helper sues Hong Kong boss for sexual harassment, seeks HK$300,000 in damages
  • High-flier schoolgirl aims to add dash of diversity to Hong Kong’s financial world as she earns place at top business school
  • Hong Kong students with special educational needs reveal challenges of studying for university entrance exams
  • Hong Kong to cut out lawyers’ role in mortgage transfers in pilot plan to protect buyers from insolvent legal firms
  • Truck driver arrested after motorcycle passenger dies in Hong Kong crash
  • Hong Kong teen challenging school policy on long hair for boys lodges complaint to equality watchdog, citing ‘gender dysphoria’
  • Where is Carrie Lam? UK-based YouTuber apologises over claims of ex-Hong Kong leader spotted at London airport, after photo evidence proves she is still in city
  • Who’s in hospital for Covid? Data shows most Hong Kong inpatients are elderly from community instead of care homes, prompting strategy rethink; 3,805 cases logged
  • Hong Kong Book Fair 2022: publishers fear for sales despite hundreds arriving early to be among first at Asia’s largest event of its kind
  • Coronavirus: what is ‘hybrid immunity’ and would Hong Kong benefit as several health experts say?
  • 6 out of 8 top scorers in Hong Kong’s university entrance exams intend to pursue studies locally to become doctors, most eyeing CUHK
  • China’s new data export rules cast doubt over Hong Kong’s role as gateway to the mainland
  • Covid and closed borders: Hong Kong has avoided inflation. But how long can this last?
  • Bringing women back into the workplace is essential for Hong Kong’s pandemic recovery
  • China’s buyers are snapping up abodes in Marco Rubio’s Florida home base, splurging US$6.1 billion on US real estate to top US list for 10th year
  • Trump-era tariffs on Chinese tech imports cost US firms US$32 billion, hurting production and jobs
  • Tech war: ASML’s second-quarter sales data shows shift away from mainland China to Taiwan and South Korea as US lobbying intensifies
  • China prepares to launch space station module for science experiments
  • China rules out ‘excessive stimulus’ to save economy, but Li Keqiang vows to refine coronavirus policy
  • Chinese car chip start-up SiEngine raises US$148 million in new funding as private equity firms invest in semiconductors
  • European Parliament vice-president urges Beijing to open ‘respectful’ dialogue with Taiwan
  • China decries ‘provocations’ after US destroyer sails through Taiwan Strait
  • China’s NetEase recruits Microsoft Xbox talent for new video games studio in Seattle
  • Hotels in Beijing banned from hosting weddings and banquets as Chinese capital tightens Covid controls
  • World first: three giant ‘corpse’ flowers bloom in Chinese botanical garden
  • Henry Kissinger warns against ‘endless confrontation’ with China
  • 4 dead, 13 injured after Tianjin gas explosion in northern China
  • China-India border: Beijing’s new highway plans near disputed territory expected to spark concern in Delhi
  • China’s approvals for new coal plants rebound amid renewed focus on energy security after last year’s power crisis: Greenpeace
  • Chinese students vow to fight penalty for distributing rainbow flags at Tsinghua University
  • Senate ‘test vote’ shows support for bill providing $52 billion to US semiconductor industry to out-compete China
  • More than just tuna: US casts a wider net to compete with China in the Pacific
  • China on cusp of joining high-income club, but slowdown raises spectre of middle-income trap
  • US adds China to list of countries where Americans face elevated risk of wrongful detention
  • China travellers reconsider flights as high oil prices see fuel surcharge surge tenfold
  • ‘Surprising shortfalls’ in China military logistics suggest lack of conflict readiness: US analysts
  • EU and China hold trade talks after delays, though few advances are made
  • Global heatwaves show urgency of adapting to changing climate
  • China cut US debt holding amid ‘risk of possible conflict’
  • Japanese, Irish leaders to keep close eye on China, agree Russia must face ‘severe consequences’
  • Pakistani men accused of gang raping US woman tourist in hotel room
  • Japan sees fewer foreign visitors even after opening border to tourists
  • Can Sri Lanka’s Ranil Wickremesinghe steer the country out of its crisis?
  • Japanese school mocked after punishing student for plucking her eyebrows
  • Thai politician criticised over claim that China has barred tourists after cannabis law
  • Unification Church’s founder was ‘close’ to Shinzo Abe’s grandfather, former chairman says
  • Coronavirus: Omicron wave swamps Australia’s hospitals; Micronesia no longer Covid-free
  • Hardline Indonesian cleric Habib Razieq Shihab released from prison
  • Failure to prosecute Japan turtle killers may embolden culprits, activists say
  • Myanmar junta troops lay landmines around churches, homes and toilets, Amnesty International says
  • Vietnam, Cambodia and Brunei join Malaysia on US trafficking blacklist
  • How the West’s sanctions on Russia are fuelling political and economic instability – and resentment – in South Asia
  • Malaysia won’t budge ‘an inch’ over Sabah as Philippine heirs chase US$15 billion claim
  • South Korea’s cheaper answer to F-35 jet makes first test flight
  • Truss and Sunak to slug it out in bid to be Britain’s next PM
  • US issues health alert warning new parents about parechovirus – signs, symptoms in babies
  • ‘Hasta la vista, baby,’ says UK’s Boris Johnson as he exits parliament
  • Europe races to cut Russian gas usage amid new Putin warning – we ‘need to be ready’, urges EU chief
  • Italy’s PM Mario Draghi sets conditions for staying in government – awaits coalition support
  • Ukraine preparing offensive on Crimea, Russia’s Black Sea Fleet: official
  • Russia state TV celebrates couple who bought car with payout from son dying in Ukraine war
  • Vladimir Putin gets Iran’s backing as US says Russia plans to annex more of Ukraine
  • As Europe sizzles, UN warns heatwaves will happen more often until 2060s
  • Ukraine war: Vladimir Putin warns EU that gas supplies could keep dwindling
  • Ex-Trump adviser Steve Bannon felt he was ‘above the law’, prosecutor says as contempt trial begins
  • Ukraine first lady Olena Zelenska visits White House during high-profile US trip
  • Why US should encourage China to lead Afghanistan’s long-term reconstruction
  • Twitter gets a win over Elon Musk with trial fast-tracked for October
  • Vladimir Putin visits Iran on first trip outside former USSR since Ukraine war began
  • Ukraine war: Zelensky sacks more intelligence service officials after numerous defections to the Russian side
  • The real answer to inflation that no one wants to hear: stop spending
  • TikTok’s global expansion stumbles as executives leave and concerns flare over data security
  • Barclays returns to Taiwan with a wholly owned subsidiary after retreat six years ago
  • China’s digital yuan gets fresh push from Beijing, but consumers see little incentive to use e-CNY
  • Mortgage boycott risks manageable for China’s banking system, but small lenders vulnerable, experts say
  • Solving inflation in 2022 means throwing out the 1980s playbook on supply chains
  • China’s top money manager adds Alibaba, Tencent, trims liquor stocks as funds beat market with portfolio tweaks
  • Alibaba, HSBC drive Hong Kong stocks to one-week high as risk appetite recovers, Fed concerns ease
  • SEC’s Gensler says it’s unclear if US and China will reach a deal to avoid stock delistings
  • More buyers of incomplete flats with stage payment schemes face higher risk of default as rising interest rates spur price falls
  • Tech giants don’t want us to know how their AI systems work or why they spread racism and untruths. An open-access alternative holds out promise
  • Is it OK to work out on weekends only? Yes, if daily exercise can’t fit your schedule, researchers show - it’s just as good for you
  • From war to dying industries, abandoned train stations have stories as varied as the structures themselves
  • Hermès Birkin and Hermès Kelly bag collector Jamie Chua, Singapore-based influencer and real-life Crazy Rich Asian, opens up her closet
  • Kim Ga-ram sacked from K-pop’s Le Sserafim over bullying claims – girl group will continue on with five members
  • Resort vibe for weekend home next to wetland in Hong Kong emphasises the expansive views that make it a birdwatcher’s paradise
  • How heroes emerge in real life to solve the pressing problems of our planet
  • Detective vs Sleuths movie review: Lau Ching-wan in wildly chaotic police thriller with urgent moral messages at its core
  • The big mistake of Philippines tourism promotion video? Assuming consumers of social media think deeply about anything
  • Singapore restaurants reimagine grandma’s home cooking and revive lost dishes from Peranakan, or Straits Chinese, cuisine
  • Diabetes emergency: a type 1 diabetic’s insulin overdose and his first-hand account of what he did to survive
  • How reading The Wisdom of the Enneagram changed the life of a former businesswoman and social enterprise founder
  • Who will be the next James Bond? 10 men – and 1 woman – in the running after Daniel Craig, from Robert Pattinson, Harry Styles and Henry Cavill, to Tom Hardy, Idris Elba and even Lashana Lynch
  • Who is Ella Yam, daughter of Hong Kong film legend Simon Yam and supermodel Qi Qi? The rising fashion icon poses in Louis Vuitton, Dior and Prada on Instagram, and is a fan of Drake and Travis Scott
  • 4 times Hwang Min-hyun proved he’s K-pop’s ‘Prince Charming’: besides his royal-looking onstage attire with Wanna One and Nu’Est, the Netflix K-drama star is apparently charming too
  • The Kardashians’ obsession with having the same baby daddy: Khloé is having a second child with cheater Tristan Thompson – just like Kim, Kourtney, Kylie also stuck it out for the sake of their kids
  • 17 female celebrity besties both on and off screen, from Marvel’s Salma Hayek and Pirates of the Caribbean star Penélope Cruz, to Ariana Grande and Elizabeth Gillies, to Zendaya and Hunter Schafer
  • Meet Princess Raiyah of Jordan, married to Roald Dahl’s grandson: the half-sister of King Abdullah II once attended a school where Queen Elizabeth and mum Queen Noor are presidents
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: NY Times: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Heat Wave Complicates Global Energy Crisis and Climate Fight
  • Putin Signals Gas Pipeline Will Resume at Reduced Levels
  • Russia’s Territorial Aims in Ukraine Have Expanded, Lavrov Says
  • Ukraine steps up attacks on key Russian targets in the south.
  • Heat Advisories and Warnings Are in Place for 100 Million Americans

  • What Driving Means for America
  • Stop Praising Shinzo Abe and His Divisive Nationalism
  • Why Russia Believes It Cannot Lose the War in Ukraine
  • Western Europe, a Climate Leader, Still Suffers From Global Warming
  • How Abortion Bans Are Restricting Miscarriage Care
  • Dan Cox, a Trump Loyalist, Wins Maryland G.O.P. Primary for Governor

  • Ranil Wickremesinghe Elected President of Sri Lanka

  • Seeking ‘Axis of Good’ Against U.S., Russia Taps Allies of Convenience

  • Chinese Banking Scandal Tests Faith in Communist Party’s Leadership

  • Virologists try to keep up with faster coronavirus evolution.

  • Your Wednesday Briefing
  • The highest-ranked Russian in women’s tennis calls the war ‘a full-blown nightmare.’

  • After Indiana Mall Shooting, One Hero but No Lasting Solution
  • California’s Kern County Has to Find Tax Revenue if Oil Drilling Ends
  • DNA on Discarded Coffee Cup Leads to Arrest in a 1975 Homicide
  • U.S. Marshals Seek Man Mistakenly Freed in Shooting of Lady Gaga’s Dog Walker
  • Abortion Provider Prepares Defamation Suit Against Indiana Attorney General
  • The Midterm Races That Give Democrats Nightmares
  • Loans Could Burn Start-Up Workers in Downturn
  • Twitter Worker Accused of Spying for Saudi Arabia Heads to Trial
  • Italy Is Haunted by the Pain of Past Economic Crises
  • Dana Canedy, Publisher of Simon & Schuster’s Flagship Imprint, Has Left the Job
  • Netflix Loses Nearly 1 Million Subscribers and Breathes a Sigh of Relief
  • Hey, Is Anybody Watching the Interns?
  • For a Revered Art House Cinema, a Season of Off Screen Turmoil
  • America’s Safety Net for Workers Hurt by Globalization Is Falling Apart
  • As crypto swoons, Anthony Scaramucci struggles to keep investors.
  • The Biden administration will challenge Mexico’s state control of its energy industry.
  • Twitter Takes Round 1
  • Inflation Climbs to 9.4 Percent in Britain, a New 40-Year High
  • Dove’s Latest Stand in the Virtue Wars
  • Hasbro Reports Rising Sales Despite Higher Prices
  • Elon Musk and Twitter to Go to Trial Over Deal In October
  • Fund Managers Show ‘Dire’ Levels of Pessimism, Poll Finds
  • Amazon Sues More Than 10,000 Facebook Groups Over Fake Reviews
  • As Baby Boomers Age, Developers Explore Housing Options for Them
  • The Cost of Extreme Heat
  • France Will Spend Nearly $10 Billion to Renationalize EDF
  • Indian Rupee Hits Weakest Level Ever Against U.S. Dollar
  • Germany Waits for Russia to Restart the Gas on Nord Stream 1
  • 3 Ways Musk Can Support Free Speech

entry.title: Guardian: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Rishi Sunak and Liz Truss reach final two of Tory leadership race as Penny Mordaunt eliminated – live
  • Treatment of UK prisoners during Covid meets UN definition of torture
  • Russia-Ukraine war: EU accuses Putin of blackmail; Moscow says peace talks ‘don’t make sense’ – live
  • Tour de France: Pogacar wins stage 17 as Vingegaard holds on and Thomas fades
  • House of the Dragon: what can we expect from the Game of Thrones prequel?
  • A Russian Sami smokes some fish: Natalya Saprunova’s best photograph
  • Quidditch changes name to quadball after JK Rowling’s trans statements
  • ‘Kafka would blush’: artist Peter Max caught in legal guardianship lawsuit
  • Backlash after Georgia school’s new logo resembles Nazi symbol
  • EU urges member states to slash gas use by 15% to counter ‘Russian blackmail’
  • US marshals hunt man wrongly released over shooting of Lady Gaga’s dog walker
  • Why Britain’s got the worst-built homes in Europe – and how we can fix them | Phineas Harper
  • ‘They hunt us like stray cats’: pro-Russia separatists step up forced conscription as losses mount
  • Statehood or independence? Puerto Rico’s status at forefront of political debate
  • Notre Dame on Fire review – high adventure ripped straight from the headlines
  • ‘Glad to have a president who can ride a bicycle’: Buttigieg dismisses Republican claims about Biden’s health
  • Hormone replacement therapy to be offered over the counter in UK
  • Beware of the cobra: why snakebites are soaring in the UK
  • Sri Lanka president vote: Ranil Wickremesinghe wins amid protests
  • Ivana Trump’s children expected at funeral mass in New York City
  • Something about Miriam Margolyes v Arnold Schwarzenegger smells funny
  • Henrik Stenson stripped of Ryder Cup captaincy with LIV deal imminent
  • Magnus Carlsen will not defend his World Chess Championship title in 2023
  • Moving The Goalposts | ‘Great togetherness’ – vibrant fans amplify the joy at Euro 2022
  • Italian PM says rebuilding trust is key before confidence vote
  • Police arrest couple over €1.6m wine heist carried out in Spain
  • EU adopts Ukrainian art exhibition left stranded in Denmark
  • I’ve been loving the women’s football – but could mixed matches be the future? | Adrian Chiles
  • I’ve spent too many hours on the phone to the DWP – I’ve reached my limit in being nice | Siobhan
  • Top 10 21st-century fantasy novels | Brian Attebery
  • How to make sweet or savoury scones – recipe | Felicity Cloake's Masterclass
  • The Spin | Garry Sobers and the life lessons of being hit for a barrage of sixes
  • Little evidence that chemical imbalance causes depression, UCL scientists find
  • Mystery of Oregon 2022 mascot Bigfoot’s missing head solved by police
  • TechScape: suspicious of TikTok? You’re not alone
  • ‘It’s all been worth it’: Jake Wightman in dreamland after emulating Cram’s gold
  • ‘It’s been a disaster’: how Boris Johnson’s docklands business hub turned into a ghost town
  • ‘We don’t want money going to private landlords’: UK music venues turn to community ownership
  • Taking on the trolls: how to support the Guardian’s women’s football coverage
  • Indonesian islanders sue cement producer for climate damages
  • Erdoğan asks Russia and Iran to back Turkey’s incursion into Syria
  • Past the parcel: how the end of free returns will change the way we shop | Sophie Benson
  • What a showstopper! How The Great British Bake off became a musical
  • Heatwave led to London firefighters’ busiest day since second world war
  • Postcards from a 40C future: what extreme heat means for the UK
  • Out of this world: Guardian writers on their favourite movies with aliens
  • Saif Gaddafi: London life of former playboy who could lead Libya revealed
  • Old cars forced off road as Europe’s clean air zones nearly double
  • A moment that changed me: a play taught me I was not undesirable – just a black woman in a white world
  • Gather round kids, let me tell you who’s REALLY responsible for the climate wars | First Dog on the Moon
  • ‘It’s a mess and I’ve never seen anything like it’: global lost luggage crisis mounts
  • ‘Back anyone but Rishi’: that’s the message behind No 10’s hints at betrayal | Andrew Murrison
  • How will Neighbours end: with a dream, a reunion – or revenge on the suits who axed it?
  • ‘Run philosophy’: the Chinese citizens seeking to leave amid Covid uncertainty
  • Erdoğan keeps Putin waiting in awkward moment ahead of Tehran talks
  • Democratic members of Congress arrested during pro-choice protest
  • Spain’s Aitana Bonmatí: ‘We want the ball. Our objective is always to play’ | Sid Lowe
  • England no longer have anything to fear despite Spain’s fresh challenge | Jonathan Liew
  • Netherlands forward Lieke Martens to miss rest of Euro 2022 with foot injury
  • Over 100m Americans under heat warnings as wildfires rage in 12 states
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia signals plan to annex fresh parts of Ukraine
  • White House warns Russia plans to annex parts of southern Ukraine
  • Ukraine set to request delay in foreign debt repayments
  • Generation moonshot: why young investors are not ready to give up on risk
  • US consumers are bending but not breaking as prices soar. Can it last?
  • The colourful Rocket man behind Donald Trump’s stalled stock debut
  • European government bonds rise as concern grows over Russian gas supplies
  • Brussels asks EU states to slash gas use by 15% starting next month
  • Sunak to face Truss for Tory leadership after Mordaunt is eliminated
  • Sunak faces a tough fight
  • Mario Draghi offers to stay as Italy’s PM if coalition partners back reform

  • FirstFT: EY boss targets $10bn boost from Silicon Valley tie-ups
  • Penny Mordaunt attacks Tory leadership rivals ahead of MPs’ final vote
  • China property: refusing to pay mortgages is a powerful political weapon
  • Rising fuel and food costs push UK inflation up to 9.4%
  • Capitulation watch!
  • All eyes on Italy while ECB considers policy response
  • The investment drought of the past two decades is catching up with us
  • Water Memories, Metropolitan Museum — the eloquent story of America’s indigenous cultures
  • Mexico has opened the door to cleaner energy, but only to some
  • Corporate jitters over Taiwan and China on the rise
  • Netflix: running up that hill in search of new subscribers
  • The curious appeal of my chatbot companion
  • Netflix loses 1mn subscribers but defections are fewer than feared
  • Boris Johnson saga may disArm UK
  • UK financial reforms/LSE: bourses are battling to stay relevant
  • Automated margin calls are another thing blockchain doesn’t have to fix
  • Inside the messy world of ESG indices
  • Capital Group adds 3 new strategies to actively managed ETFs
  • Eurozone leaders are pushing the ECB into murky waters
  • Wall Street stocks rally as earnings season enters full swing
  • Ukraine grain deal and Syria top agenda for Putin’s Iran-Turkey talks
  • Ukraine and Russia near deal to end blockade of grain exports
  • Buyout debt: The moving business gets back to work
  • Halliburton boss says oilfield service shortages likely to worsen
  • Founders of failed UK energy supplier in line for £50mn
  • Planemakers step up hydrogen tests in push to clean aviation
  • UK public services under strain as temperature tops 40C
  • Climate change should be central to the UK’s leadership contest
  • US airlines: loyalty schemes are revenue gold
  • Aviation sector needs to give up the blame game
  • Do we qualify for divorce mediation vouchers?
  • Fast fashion is slowing down — for its own good
  • China’s zero-Covid policy risks economic damage spiralling
  • Jefferies: focus on investment banking comes amid market retreat

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-20 15:25PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Rishi Sunak or Liz Truss to become Britain’s next prime minister
  • Unfair to blame Labor for neglect of environment
  • ‘Doctors didn’t want me to fight’: Tszyu overcomes ear surgery and Horn
  • Penelope Cruz plays torturer-in-chief in this comedic delight
  • TPG Telecom slams Berejiklian claims on network-sharing deal
  • What if there aren’t enough home buyers ready to pay what a seller is asking?
  • Why are one in four Sydney homes are being pulled from auction?
  • Breaking bad: How BlackRock lost $1.7 trillion dollars in six months
  • From the Archives, 1942: Remembering the war on the Kokoda Track
  • In the Herald: July 21, 1955

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
